turns.content: Good afternoon, and welcome to the NVIDIA Corp. Q1 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered an outstanding quarter with results exceeding our expectations across every segment. In Q1 2024, We saw 26% growth in GeForce RTX, well above our internal targets. Our inference scaling strategy continues to gain traction, with GeForce RTX showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in inference scaling because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from data center GPU validate our approach. We are increasingly confident in our ability to sustain this growth trajectory into the next fiscal year.

Thank you, Jensen. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2024 was $36.4 billion, representing growth of 3% year over year. Our data center revenue reached $24.8 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin was 33.3%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $21.1 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $5 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For H100, we saw 22% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the data center revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Erik. Great question. On data center revenue, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in AI demand has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect GeForce RTX to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Colette for the specific numbers.
I wanted to ask about supply constraints. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Keith. The backlog trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in H100?

Thanks, Wamsi. Great question. On gross margin, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in inference scaling has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect H100 to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Colette for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the NVIDIA Corp. Q2 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered an outstanding quarter with results exceeding our expectations across every segment. In Q2 2024, Revenue grew 26% year over year, significantly ahead of consensus estimates. Our sovereign AI strategy continues to gain traction, with H100 showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in sovereign AI because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from inference validate our approach. Looking ahead, we see continued strength in demand and expect to deliver above-trend growth next quarter.

Thank you, Jensen. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2024 was $30.9 billion, representing growth of 20% year over year. Our data center revenue reached $43.6 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin expanded 43.5%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $26.8 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $14 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For H100, we saw 23% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Great, thanks. On DGX, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Raimo. The backlog trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Brad. The gross margin trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On H100, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Shannon. Great question. On data center revenue, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in sovereign AI has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect H100 to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Colette for the specific numbers.
I wanted to ask about AI demand. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Amit. The gross margin trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Brent. Great question. On backlog, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in sovereign AI has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect DGX to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Colette for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the NVIDIA Corp. Q3 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

Our results this quarter reflect a more difficult operating environment than we anticipated. In Q3 2024, We saw a 11% decline in DGX as customers delayed purchasing decisions amid macro uncertainty. Our supply constraints strategy continues to gain traction, with DGX showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in supply constraints because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from data center GPU validate our approach. Given the current visibility, we are revising our guidance range to reflect a more conservative scenario.

Thank you, Jensen. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $75.9 billion, representing a decline of 0% year over year. Our data center revenue reached $11.2 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 40.8%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $28.4 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $5 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For H100, we saw 12% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Mark. The backlog trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On DGX, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Wamsi. Great question. On gaming revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position DGX for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect DGX to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Colette for the specific numbers.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Raimo. Great question. On backlog, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position A100 for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect A100 to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Colette for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the NVIDIA Corp. Q4 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

Our performance this quarter reflects steady execution against our strategic priorities. In Q4 2024, data center revenue was largely stable compared to the prior quarter, reflecting balanced supply and demand dynamics. Our supply constraints strategy continues to gain traction, with DGX showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in supply constraints because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from AI training validate our approach. We are maintaining our full-year guidance and continue to invest in long-term growth opportunities.

Thank you, Jensen. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q4 2024 was $48.5 billion, representing a decline of 1% year over year. Our data center revenue reached $50.6 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 41.3%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $9.8 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $7 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For H100, we saw 7% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Great, thanks. On Grace Hopper, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Amit. The gross margin trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On H100, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Erik. Great question. On gaming revenue, we are pleased with the progress on AI demand and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect H100 to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Colette for the specific numbers.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the gaming revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Keith. Great question. On gaming revenue, we are pleased with the progress on supply constraints and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect H100 to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Colette for the specific numbers.
Great, thanks. On GeForce RTX, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Shannon. The gaming revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Sure, Kash. The data center revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.

Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

Sure, Mark. The data center revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.

Thanks, Brent. Great question. On gaming revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position GeForce RTX for recovery. Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.
